name: rank-bm25 (Python BM25 Implementation)

performance.notes: Pure Python; linear search over corpus; bm25s is faster alternative for large corpora

agent_readiness.notes: Lightweight BM25 retrieval for RAG baseline. from rank_bm25 import BM25Okapi. corpus = [['python', 'programming', 'language'], ['python', 'snake', 'reptile']]. bm25 = BM25Okapi(corpus). scores = bm25.get_scores(['python', 'language']). top_n = bm25.get_top_n(['python', 'language'], corpus, n=3). Variants: BM25Okapi (default), BM25L (handles long docs better), BM25Plus (fixes BM25 zero floor issue). tokenization is manual — caller provides token lists. No persistence — rebuild from corpus each time. Minimal dependencies (numpy only). bm25.doc_freqs, bm25.idf.

gotchas: Manual tokenization — must preprocess text yourself (lowercase, stem, stop words)
No persistence — must rebuild BM25 index on every restart
Linear search over corpus — not suitable for millions of documents (use bm25s or Elasticsearch)
best_when: RAG baseline retrieval or hybrid retrieval (BM25 + dense vectors)
Small to medium corpora (<100K documents) where low latency isn't critical
Research baselines and ablation studies comparing BM25 vs dense retrieval
Quick keyword search without setting up full-text search infrastructure
avoid_when: Large corpora (> 100K docs) — use Elasticsearch or bm25s instead
Production search requiring persistence and updates
When semantic search is needed (use sentence-transformers + FAISS)
summary: rank-bm25 provides lightweight BM25 retrieval. BM25Okapi(tokenized_corpus). get_scores(query_tokens). get_top_n(query, corpus, n=5). Variants: BM25L, BM25Plus. Numpy-only dependency. Apache-2.0 by Dorian Brown. Best for RAG baseline and hybrid retrieval on small corpora. Manual tokenization required. No persistence. Linear search. Use bm25s for large corpora speed.
